NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SALLY LIU MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITIES. CAUSES OF ACTION UNDER SFRO 37.10B, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND UCL VIOLATION ARE "MIXED" CAUSES OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. SEE MANN V. QUALITY OLD TIME (2004) 120 CAL.APP.4TH 90 AND WALLACE V. MCCUBBIN (2011) 196 CAL.APP.4TH 1169. MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Edward Higginbotham, Esq. (415-581-0885) for Plaintiff/Opposing party Jose Ceron.
Gregory Haines, Esq. (415-546-0777) for Defendant/moving party Sally Liu.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SALLY LIU MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Negligence cause of action does not arise out of protected activities. Causes of action under SFRO 37.10B, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and UCL violation are "mixed" causes of action and Plaintiffs have shown probability of prevailing on the merits. See Mann v. Quality Old Time (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 90 and Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Malicious prosecution cause of action arises out of protected activity. Plaintiffs have shown probability of prevailing on the merits. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson II Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Continued To Mar-14-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Higginbotham, Esq. (415-581-0885) for Plaintiff/Opposing party Jose Ceron.
Gregory Haines, Esq. (415-546-0777) for Defendant/moving party Sally Liu.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SALLY LIU's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is continued to March 14, 2016. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFRO 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of opposition to department 501 no later than February 26, 2016 by 4:00 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
